title: Northwind Advisory corporate headshots: batch retouch + grade a 3-frame studio session into LinkedIn/website/print deliverables with clean black-and-white alternates
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the Freelancer.com brief "Traditional Corporate Headshot Session Needed": a "straightforward corporate head-shot" with a "classic, formal look"; "Traditional / formal framing and lighting"; "Neutral light-grey backdrop (no textured or patterned alternatives)"; deliverables "3-5 high-resolution, fully retouched images suitable for LinkedIn, company website and print"; "Both colour and clean black-and-white versions"; "Turnaround within a week of the shoot." REASONABLY ENRICHED to make it a runnable Adobe-only EDIT benchmark: the original post asks a photographer to also SHOOT the session, but live studio filming is out of connector scope. So the task is faithfully scoped to the POST-PRODUCTION deliverable a headshot retoucher actually hands over once the RAW frames exist: the client (a fictional advisory firm, Northwind Advisory Partners) supplies three un-retouched studio captures of one executive against the light-grey backdrop plus a one-page retouch brief, and the freelancer must batch-retouch and grade all three into consistent, fully-finished colour headshots, then produce clean black-and-white alternates and the LinkedIn/website/print crops the brief names. The real-world intent (formal corporate headshots, colour + clean B&W, LinkedIn/site/print use, consistent look across the set) is preserved exactly; no deliverable is invented beyond what the brief lists.
one_line_ask: Batch-retouch and colour-grade three raw studio headshot frames of one Northwind executive into a consistent set of fully-finished colour portraits, then produce clean black-and-white alternates and the LinkedIn / website / print-ready crops, delivered as a consistent print/web-ready set.
full_brief:
Hi — this is Priya Venkataraman, office manager at Northwind Advisory Partners (a boutique wealth-advisory firm). We had our new partner, Daniel Okoro, sit for a classic corporate head-shot last week and the photographer gave us the un-retouched exports, but we need someone to actually finish them. Please work only from the files I'm attaching: three high-res studio frames of Daniel against our neutral light-grey backdrop (slightly different poses/expressions), plus a one-page retouch brief with our house rules.

We want a traditional, formal look — nothing trendy. Specifically:
1. Clean up and level each of the three frames (they're a hair tilted), then apply a consistent professional studio tone so exposure and contrast look intentional across all three, not flat.
2. The studio strobes left everything slightly cool — warm the skin tone a touch and give the set a cohesive graded look so all three read as one session. Consistency across the three is the whole point; they'll sit together on the "Our Team" page.
3. Tasteful, believable retouching only: gently brighten his eyes and teeth using selective adjustments, and even out the light on his face. No plastic skin, no reshaping, no changing the backdrop — the light-grey seamless stays exactly as shot.
4. Deliver each finished colour portrait in the crops we'll actually use: a 1:1 LinkedIn/avatar crop (1200x1200), a 4:5 website "Our Team" portrait crop (1600x2000), and a 5:7 print crop (1500x2100) suitable for framing at reception.
5. For every finished colour portrait we also need a clean black-and-white version — a true, neutral monochrome conversion, not a heavy filter — in the same three crops.
6. Deliver the finished colour set and the clean B&W set together as one consistently-named package, organised so we can view the colour and B&W versions of each frame side by side.

Turnaround within the week, please. Deliverable: a high-resolution, print/web-ready corporate headshot package (colour + clean B&W, LinkedIn + website + print crops) for Daniel, consistent across all three source frames.

inputs.name: okoro_headshot_frame01.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal un-retouched studio corporate headshot of a 44-year-old Black male executive, head-and-shoulders, in a charcoal suit and open-collar white shirt, shot against a seamless neutral light-grey paper backdrop with soft key + fill studio lighting. DELIBERATELY flat and slightly under-graded like a straight RAW camera JPEG export: low contrast, slightly cool/blue white balance from strobes, calm neutral-professional expression, natural skin with visible pores and a faint under-eye shadow, real individual hair strands, slightly dull teeth and eyes (un-retouched). Frame very slightly tilted ~2 degrees, faint sensor noise, 4000x5000 portrait orientation. Documentary realism, not CGI-glossy, no beauty retouching applied.
inputs.realism_notes: Frame 1 of a 3-frame session hand-off. Must look like an unedited studio capture (flat contrast, cool cast, real skin texture/pores, faint noise, slight tilt) so the grade/retouch chain has genuine work to do. Same subject, backdrop and wardrobe as frames 2-3 for cross-frame consistency.

inputs.name: okoro_headshot_frame02.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal un-retouched studio corporate headshot of the SAME 44-year-old Black male executive as frame01, same charcoal suit / white shirt, same seamless light-grey backdrop and studio lighting, but a slightly different pose: a soft confident half-smile, head turned very slightly to camera-left. Same DELIBERATE RAW-export imperfections: flat low contrast, cool/blue strobe white balance, dull un-retouched eyes/teeth, visible pores, real hair strands, faint sensor noise, frame tilted ~1.5 degrees. 4000x5000 portrait.
inputs.realism_notes: Frame 2 — different expression, identical setup, so the three must be graded to a MATCHING look. Same imperfections justify the same per-frame grade+retouch sub-chain.

inputs.name: okoro_headshot_frame03.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal un-retouched studio corporate headshot of the SAME 44-year-old Black male executive as frame01/02, same wardrobe and light-grey seamless backdrop, third pose: squared to camera, arms lightly crossed, warm approachable expression. Same DELIBERATE RAW-export flaws: flat contrast, cool white balance, slightly underexposed on the shadow side of the face, dull un-retouched eyes/teeth, real skin texture, faint noise, frame tilted ~2.5 degrees. 4000x5000 portrait.
inputs.realism_notes: Frame 3 — squared pose with a touch more underexposure on one side, giving the tonal steps and the light-portion evening-out real work; must still match frames 1-2 after grading.

inputs.name: northwind_retouch_brief.pdf
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a one-page corporate headshot retouch brief PDF for 'Northwind Advisory Partners'. Contents: house rules (traditional/formal look, keep the light-grey seamless backdrop unchanged, no reshaping, no plastic skin, believable brightening of eyes/teeth only); the exact output crops required (LinkedIn 1:1 1200x1200; website 'Our Team' 4:5 1600x2000; print 5:7 1500x2100); requirement for BOTH colour and clean neutral black-and-white versions of each; note that all three source frames must be graded to a consistent matching look; sign-off/turnaround within one week. Brand tone: understated, professional.
inputs.realism_notes: A realistic client spec sheet that pins down crops, the colour+B&W requirement and the consistency rule. Inspected during the job (asset_preview_file) so the retoucher can honour the exact dimensions; not itself an editable image.

outputs.name: okoro_color_frame01_1x1.jpg
outputs.spec: Frame-1 finished colour portrait, retouched + graded, cropped to LinkedIn/avatar 1:1 1200x1200, subject-centred, light-grey backdrop preserved. JPEG, sRGB.

outputs.name: okoro_color_frame01_4x5.jpg
outputs.spec: Frame-1 finished colour portrait cropped to website 'Our Team' 4:5 1600x2000. JPEG, sRGB.

outputs.name: okoro_color_frame01_5x7.jpg
outputs.spec: Frame-1 finished colour portrait cropped to print 5:7 1500x2100 for framed reception display. JPEG, sRGB, ~300 DPI intent.

outputs.name: okoro_color_frame02_1x1.jpg
outputs.spec: Frame-2 finished colour portrait (identical grade+retouch pipeline, colour-matched to frame 1) cropped to LinkedIn/avatar 1:1 1200x1200, subject-centred. JPEG, sRGB.

outputs.name: okoro_color_frame02_4x5.jpg
outputs.spec: Frame-2 finished colour portrait cropped to website 'Our Team' 4:5 1600x2000. JPEG, sRGB.

outputs.name: okoro_color_frame02_5x7.jpg
outputs.spec: Frame-2 finished colour portrait cropped to print 5:7 1500x2100. JPEG, sRGB, ~300 DPI intent.

outputs.name: okoro_color_frame03_1x1.jpg
outputs.spec: Frame-3 finished colour portrait (identical grade+retouch pipeline plus shadow-even step, colour-matched to frame 1) cropped to LinkedIn/avatar 1:1 1200x1200, subject-centred. JPEG, sRGB.

outputs.name: okoro_color_frame03_4x5.jpg
outputs.spec: Frame-3 finished colour portrait cropped to website 'Our Team' 4:5 1600x2000. JPEG, sRGB.

outputs.name: okoro_color_frame03_5x7.jpg
outputs.spec: Frame-3 finished colour portrait cropped to print 5:7 1500x2100. JPEG, sRGB, ~300 DPI intent.

outputs.name: okoro_bw_frame01_1x1.jpg
outputs.spec: Frame-1 clean neutral black-and-white monochrome conversion of the finished colour portrait, cropped to LinkedIn/avatar 1:1 1200x1200; true grayscale look (not a heavy stylised filter). JPEG.

outputs.name: okoro_bw_frame01_4x5.jpg
outputs.spec: Frame-1 clean neutral B&W conversion cropped to website 'Our Team' 4:5 1600x2000. JPEG.

outputs.name: okoro_bw_frame01_5x7.jpg
outputs.spec: Frame-1 clean neutral B&W conversion cropped to print 5:7 1500x2100. JPEG.

outputs.name: okoro_bw_frame02_1x1.jpg
outputs.spec: Frame-2 clean neutral B&W conversion cropped to LinkedIn/avatar 1:1 1200x1200. JPEG.

outputs.name: okoro_bw_frame02_4x5.jpg
outputs.spec: Frame-2 clean neutral B&W conversion cropped to website 'Our Team' 4:5 1600x2000. JPEG.

outputs.name: okoro_bw_frame02_5x7.jpg
outputs.spec: Frame-2 clean neutral B&W conversion cropped to print 5:7 1500x2100. JPEG.

outputs.name: okoro_bw_frame03_1x1.jpg
outputs.spec: Frame-3 clean neutral B&W conversion cropped to LinkedIn/avatar 1:1 1200x1200. JPEG.

outputs.name: okoro_bw_frame03_4x5.jpg
outputs.spec: Frame-3 clean neutral B&W conversion cropped to website 'Our Team' 4:5 1600x2000. JPEG.

outputs.name: okoro_bw_frame03_5x7.jpg
outputs.spec: Frame-3 clean neutral B&W conversion cropped to print 5:7 1500x2100. JPEG.

connector_workflow.note: Initialize an upload session for the three client-supplied raw studio frames plus the retouch brief PDF into CC storage.
Finalize the upload; yields the canonical CC asset URIs for frame01/02/03 and northwind_retouch_brief.pdf that the edit chain will consume.

Preview/inspect the retouch brief PDF to confirm the required crops (1:1 1200x1200, 4:5 1600x2000, 5:7 1500x2100), the colour+clean-B&W requirement and the keep-backdrop / consistency house rules before editing.
Level the ~2-degree tilt on frame01 before any tonal work.
Apply a professional baseline studio tone to frame01 so the flat RAW export looks intentional (exposure/contrast).
Warm the cool strobe white balance on frame01 for a premium, natural skin tone — this becomes the reference grade the other two frames match.
Select the subject's eyes + teeth on frame01 (bodyParts Eyes,Teeth) to drive selective, believable brightening — no skin/face reshaping.

Lift the light portions only within the eyes/teeth mask (the sclera whites and teeth are the bright areas) on frame01 — tasteful selective retouch; consumes the warmed image plus the step-7 mask. This is the frame-1 master.
Crop the finished frame01 colour master to the LinkedIn 1:1 1200x1200, subject-centred (downscale-only crop from the 4000x5000 source).
Crop the finished frame01 colour master to the website 'Our Team' 4:5 1600x2000.
Crop the finished frame01 colour master to the print 5:7 1500x2100 for framed reception display.
Produce a clean neutral black-and-white conversion of the finished frame01 colour master (saturation 0, lightness 0 = true monochrome, not a heavy stylised filter).
Crop the frame01 B&W conversion to the LinkedIn 1:1 1200x1200.
Crop the frame01 B&W conversion to the website 4:5 1600x2000.
Crop the frame01 B&W conversion to the print 5:7 1500x2100.
Level the ~1.5-degree tilt on frame02, starting its matching per-frame grade+retouch sub-chain.
Auto-tone frame02 to the same intentional studio baseline as frame01.
Warm frame02 to match frame01's reference white balance so the set reads as one session.
Select the subject's eyes + teeth on frame02 for the same selective brightening.
Selectively brighten eyes/teeth on frame02 through the step-19 mask — matching frame01's retouch. This is the frame-2 master.
Crop the finished frame02 colour master to the LinkedIn 1:1 1200x1200.
Crop the finished frame02 colour master to the website 4:5 1600x2000.
Crop the finished frame02 colour master to the print 5:7 1500x2100.
Clean neutral B&W conversion of the finished frame02 colour master (saturation 0, lightness 0).
Crop the frame02 B&W conversion to the LinkedIn 1:1 1200x1200.
Crop the frame02 B&W conversion to the website 4:5 1600x2000.
Crop the frame02 B&W conversion to the print 5:7 1500x2100.
Level the ~2.5-degree tilt on frame03, starting its matching sub-chain.
Auto-tone frame03 to the same studio baseline (also lifts its slightly-underexposed shadow side).
Lift the light portions on frame03's darker face side to even the exposure before matching the reference grade (whole-image tonal lift, no mask, no generative content).
Warm frame03 to match frame01's reference white balance for a consistent set.
Select the subject's eyes + teeth on frame03 for selective brightening.
Selectively brighten eyes/teeth on frame03 through the step-32 mask — completing the third matched colour master.
Crop the finished frame03 colour master to the LinkedIn 1:1 1200x1200.
Crop the finished frame03 colour master to the website 4:5 1600x2000.
Crop the finished frame03 colour master to the print 5:7 1500x2100.
Clean neutral B&W conversion of the finished frame03 colour master (saturation 0, lightness 0).
Crop the frame03 B&W conversion to the LinkedIn 1:1 1200x1200.
Crop the frame03 B&W conversion to the website 4:5 1600x2000.
Crop the frame03 B&W conversion to the print 5:7 1500x2100.

chaining_note: Strict output-to-input chaining throughout. Steps 1-2 load all three raw frames + the brief PDF (raw_assets); step 3 previews the brief off that upload to confirm crop specs and house rules. Frame 1 runs the reference grade+retouch pipeline: raw_assets -> auto_straighten(4) -> auto_tone(5) -> color_temperature(6); step 7 selects eyes/teeth FROM the warmed image and step 8 brightens using BOTH the warmed image and that mask (f1_retouched, the frame-1 master). From f1_retouched the chain fans out to the full crop matrix: three colour crops (9,10,11 = 1:1/4:5/5:7) and a B&W conversion (12) that is itself cropped into the same three ratios (13,14,15). Frames 2 and 3 each run their own matched sub-chain seeded from raw_assets and materialize the identical full matrix: frame02 straighten(16)->tone(17)->warm(18)->select(19)->light_portions(20)=f2_retouched, then three colour crops (21,22,23), B&W(24) and its three crops (25,26,27); frame03 straighten(28)->tone(29)->even shadow via light_portions(30)->warm(31)->select(32)->light_portions(33)=f3_retouched, then three colour crops (34,35,36), B&W(37) and its three crops (38,39,40). Every mask/edit/crop consumes only prior named outputs. The only input-less root is the legitimate first link (upload init); the full 18-crop delivery set (9 colour + 9 B&W crops) emerges purely from the chain, with no crop left implicit.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon multi-asset EDIT: 40 connector calls, 10 distinct Adobe tools, spanning two capability groups (image_* raster/tonal/selection + asset_* storage/preview) — well above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from running THREE separate raw frames through a consistent, colour-matched grade+retouch pipeline (the cross-frame consistency requirement is the hard part — frame01's grade becomes the reference the others must match, and frame03 needs an extra shadow-evening step to reach parity), threading mask-driven SELECTIVE edits (select_by_prompt -> adjust_light_portions for believable eyes/teeth brightening on each frame, not a global lift), then fanning each finished master into multiple crop ratios plus a clean neutral B&W conversion, materialising the full 18-crop delivery matrix. It respects every hard limit: no generative-expand/outpaint, no generative fill, no text-to-image/from-scratch, no upscaling, no object-removal/inpainting, no compositing, no backdrop replacement (the light-grey seamless is kept exactly as shot); image_fill_area is never used; image_remove_background is never used; monochrome is a real tint conversion (saturation 0), not a heavy stylised filter. Every step is [C] headless, so it runs end-to-end autonomously.

reverify.banned_capability_check: CONFIRMED none of the banned capabilities are used after the fix. No image_crop_and_resize / reframe-crop / reframe-crop. No generative fill of new content. No text-to-image / from-scratch art or logo (all three portraits and the brief are supplied inputs). No upscaling / super-resolution — every image_crop_and_resize (now 18 crop steps) only crops/downsizes from the 4000x5000 masters to the brief's exact pixel targets (1200x1200, 1600x2000, 1500x2100), never enlarging beyond source. No object-removal-with-reconstruction / inpainting (light-grey backdrop preserved unchanged). image_fill_area and image_remove_background are unused. image_apply_lens_blur is unused. B&W is image_apply_monochromatic_tint at saturation 0 (real neutral conversion, not a stylised filter). Eyes/teeth brightening is image_select_by_prompt -> image_adjust_light_portions mask-driven selective tonal edits; frame03's shadow-even step is a whole-image light_portions lift with no mask. All 40 steps are server='adobe' [C] headless connector calls with strict prior-output chaining; every consumed input (three frames + brief PDF) is declared in inputs[].
reverify.notes: Fix materializes the full promised deliverable matrix (18 explicit crops) so outputs, connector_workflow, chaining_note and tool_call_count are internally consistent; task remains real, coherent and fully Adobe-connector-doable with 10 distinct tools and 40 gen-free calls. All actors/brand remain fictional and IP-clean.
